characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: One of our two protagonists, we first meet Vorenus as a centurion of the 13th Legion in Gaul who has not seen home nor his family for 8 years. After he returns, he is reunited with his family but finds himself caught up in the civil wars of Caesar and Pompey in order to provide for them. A strict Stoic and Catonian, Vorenus believes in honor, honesty, and piety. But as the dissembling nature of Rome grows more and more clear, he finds himself more and more lost. 10-Minute Retirement: Happens soon after his return from Gaul. He ends up re-enlisting in the legions in order to provide, although he at least goes in asevocati,which has a bit more prestige and a better contract. Above the Influence: As part of his character development with his wife. Late in the first season, he's ordered by a female superior to make love to her, and goes to obey since he's a soldier whomustobey or risk penalty. However, he finds himself unable to go through with it and tells her to punish him if he will, but he won't. The female superior promptly dismisses him, and he sendsPulloin to handle her instead. Arranged Marriage: He was engaged to Niobe from a young age, and they had two children before he had to leave for the Gaul campaign. While it's often rocky between them, they get better over time.

characters.name: Lucius Vorenus
characters.description: One of our two protagonists, we first meet Vorenus as a centurion of the 13th Legion in Gaul who has not seen home nor his family for 8 years. After he returns, he is reunited with his family but finds himself caught up in the civil wars of Caesar and Pompey in order to provide for them. A strict Stoic and Catonian, Vorenus believes in honor, honesty, and piety. But as the dissembling nature of Rome grows more and more clear, he finds himself more and more lost. 10-Minute Retirement: Happens soon after his return from Gaul. He ends up re-enlisting in the legions in order to provide, although he at least goes in asevocati,which has a bit more prestige and a better contract. Above the Influence: As part of his character development with his wife. Late in the first season, he's ordered by a female superior to make love to her, and goes to obey since he's a soldier whomustobey or risk penalty. However, he finds himself unable to go through with it and tells her to punish him if he will, but he won't. The female superior promptly dismisses him, and he sendsPulloin to handle her instead. Arranged Marriage: He was engaged to Niobe from a young age, and they had two children before he had to leave for the Gaul campaign. While it's often rocky between them, they get better over time.

characters.name: Titus Pullo
characters.description: The other protagonist of the series, Pullo is the image of a grizzled Roman soldier - he loves the legions, the ladies, and living while he can. In the premiere, he and Vorenus are thrown together on a mission, and after bonding with his family he becomes Vorenus's right hand and uncle to his children, allowing him to explore a softer side. While Vorenus embodies the struggles of the middle-class Roman patriarch, Pullo embodies the struggles of the working class Roman who is two steps from poverty - barely able to think of the Republic in the struggle to keep himself alive. As the wars of Caesar continue, he finds himself desperate to protect his wife Eirene, and to stand by his promise to protect Lucius's family. The Alcoholic: Loves a good drink. In the pilot, he's introduced getting punished for breaking formation because he's been drinking too much. Anti-Hero: He's a murderer with a hair-trigger temper, but he's one of our heroes.Cleopatra: Is he a good man?Vorenus: ...Define "good." Apologetic Attacker: Every assassination victim should be lucky enough for someone like Pullo to show up at his door.Cicero:(trembling)Please... I... give me a few moments.Pullo:(sheathes his sword)Whenever you're ready.When Cicero's finally ready, Pullo advises him to kneel down, so that Pullo can make it as quick and painless as possible. He also tells Cicero's distraught secretary, Tiro, that he might want to look away (to his credit, Tiro doesn't).

characters.name: Niobe
characters.description: Vorenus' wife through anArranged Marriageand mother of two daughters,both named Vorena. At the start of the series she has been in charge of the Voreni household for eight years while her husband was at war in Gaul, and under the impression that he was dead for the last year, meaning they lacked his pay and were struggling. Unbeknownst to Vorenus, she is carrying a dark secret - when she thought Vorenus was dead, and after her sister failed to give her husband Evander a child, Evander came over to her house and the two had relations, resulting in a son. In the present, Niobe has settled on passing the baby off as her eldest daughter's, conceived with a cattle driver boy she's dating, and enlisting her daughters' help to hide the truth from Vorenus. But as time goes on, the secret becomes harder and harder to keep. Noble, dutiful, but torn by circumstance, Niobe represents the difficulties of being a matriarch in Roman society, and how its laws don't always hold up to complex reality... Accidental Adultery: Explored at length. Technically, she didn't cheat, as she fully thought Lucius was dead at the time. However, she did help her brother-in-law cheat on her sister, which her sister was extremely hurt by. The characters admit that it's conceivable Vorenus might forgiveherconsidering the circumstances and if he was in a good enough mood. But they also are confident that even if things went very well, he'd feel very obligated to kill the boy - an illegitimate male heir is both humiliating and disastrous for familial succession - and they're not willing to risk his life like that. Aloof Big Sister: To Lyde.

characters.name: Eirene
characters.description: A Germanic slave that Vorenus and Pullo rescue from a band of Pompeian soldiers early in Season 1, Eirene is from then on employed as a slave - and later servant - of the Vorenus household. Soon after, she finds herself the object of Pullo's affections, and after a good while she consents to becomes his wife. Beautiful, reticent, and in very tough circumstances, she symbolizes the place of female slaves in Roman times, as her choices are limited by her very few options, her negligible skills, andthe fact that her beauty makes her a target. Beautiful Slave Girl: Plays the part of one, as a beautiful female slave has more uses than a normal-looking one. While the Voreni aren't interested in her that way - Lucius is devoted to making his marriage work and Pullo does not (forcefully) compel his partners - it's implied that she's had a very hard time of it before coming into their service because a beautiful slave girl is the target of unwanted affection. Betty: To Gaia's Veronica in the second season as Gaia competes for Pullo's attention. Cute Mute: Doesn't speak much at first, since she doesn't speak much Latin. Over time she becomes more fluent. Damsel in Distress: How she is introduced. Pompey's men capture herfor their own use, andPullo frees her.

characters.name: The Voreni Children
characters.description: The three alleged children of the house of Vorenus, with the Vorenas his alleged daughters and Lucius his alleged grandson. Unbeknownst to Vorenus, Lucius is actually Niobe's son by her brother-in-law Evander, conceived in a moment of weakness when Niobe thought he was dead. They have since settled on passing Lucius off as Vorena the Elder's child by a cattle drover in order to protect him. At the series' start, Lucius returns to them after eight years and begins parenting again, encountering significant friction as he tries to get used to his wife and his family's needs. When the second season starts, they are abducted and sold into slavery for a time, which affects their lives disastrously. Through them, Lucius's struggle to be a good Roman patriarch are embodied. Death Glare: Vorena the Younger develops one, according to Pullo, who compares it to “Medusa on the rag”. Defiled Forever: When Vorena the Elder is enslaved in the second season she's used as a prostitute, which both makes Lucius very upset but also makes it difficult for him to find a match for her, since he thinks that history will be found out and ruin a marriage.

characters.name: Lyde
characters.description: Niobe's younger sister and wife to Greek fishmonger Evander. At series' start, she is helping hide the family'sDark Secret- her husband impregnated Niobe a year previously when Niobe thought Vorenus was dead and Lyde was failing to conceive children. In the present, they are all now complicit in hiding this secret from Vorenus to protect the life of the child and even Niobe if it's found out. However, the fact of her husband's betrayal weighs on her, and as time goes on she finds it harder to bear the lie... Despair Event Horizon:Is very hurt by her husband's death. The Glorious War of Sisterly Rivalry: Is (quite reasonably) upset with Niobe. Over time she makes efforts to mend the relationship, but it's clear the betrayal of her husband and sister hurt a lot. In Vino Veritas: On one occasion she nearly spills the secret because she's drank too much. From then on she controls her intake.

characters.name: Evander Pulchio
characters.description: Lyde's husband, a Greek fish dealer. While he appears harmless on the outside, it's revealed that he had relations with Niobe a year prior when she thought Vorenus was dead, resulting in a child they have since passed off as Vorena the Elder's. While he's committed to perpetuating the lie to protect the baby, over time he finds it harder and harder to stick to it, and the pain of betraying his wife makes her more reluctant to protect him... Cold-Blooded Torture:When Pullo suspects the truth, he and Octavian kidnap him and torture him until he talks. It takes a long time and hurts a lot. Get It Over With:Faced with torture he immediately confesses to sleeping with Niobe and tells them to go ahead and kill him. Pullo is entirely willing to do so, but Octavian realises Pulchio isonly saying this to avoid revealing something elseunder torture. Eventually he breaks and pleads with them to put him out of his misery. Infraction Distraction:He confesses to his affair with Niobe to protect his son. Unfortunately Octavian isn't fooled and insists that Pullo keep torturing him until he breaks. Law of Inverse Fertility: Was failing to have children with Lyde and then went over and had relations with Niobe in a moment of weakness on her part. While he's happy keeping the lie to protect the child, over time it becomes clear that he still perceives the child as his and chafes at having to pretend otherwise...

characters.name: Julius Caesar
characters.description: While he takes pains to appear unbothered, and judiciously avoids saying he wants to be supreme ruler in front of even his closest partners, this version of Caesar is driven entirely by ambition, bent entirely on twisting the apparatus of the Republic into something he controls utterly. While this involves upsetting the ruling nobles, it also involves uplifting the common plebians and soldiers, which frequently puts him into contact with Lucius and Pullo. Over the course of the series, the legacy of Caesar mirrors theirs, and by series' end both have settled into a rough equilibrium. Accidental Hero: Accidentally rescues Vorenus's family by arriving right when gangsters were coming to attack. The Ace: When it comes to being a dictator anyway; none of his contemporaries or successors can match him in statecraftandwarfare. While Octavian is a more ruthless statesman, he lacks any kind of military skill. While Antony is a soldier's soldier loved by his legions, he is crass, a terrible statesmen and hated by the Roman political elite. Affably Evil: His manipulative and ruthless moments are very easy to overlook thanks to his magnetic and soft-spoken charisma. Caesar is extremely good at not appearing hurtful. Antagonist in Mourning: When his long-term rival for leadership Pompey is finally killed, he's quite affected, as he was still a close peer and respected friend, and the ones who killed him undermined his power by doing so.Caesar:He was a consul of Rome!

characters.name: Octavian
characters.description: One of the main protagonists, and future Emperor Augustus Julius Caesar, the first great Roman emperor. Over the course of this series, he starts out first as a young teenager and eventually a young man, well before most of his reign. Brilliant yet isolated, and with a very dysfunctional immediate family, he latches on to his uncle as a means of gaining influence and putting his ambitious brain to work, and eventually makes it to the top. While the real Augustus was lauded far and wide as a supremely virtuous and loving man, this rendition of the man is notably cold and detached, suggesting that even the most virtuous of Roman emperors must still needs be a tough and distant man. 0% Approval Rating: Is at risk of this in the later episodes when Mark Antony and Cleopatra cuts off Egyptian grain shipments to Rome, causing a famine. While Octavian would never do this, people don't believe Mark Antony would either and naturally fall to him as the cause. Break the Cutie: Happens on occasion. His mother, in particular, is focused on toughening him up in order to make a proper man, which has very mixed results. Break the Haughty: Introduced in the pilot as a patrician snob with little regard for the lower classes or even his household slaves. His subsequent experience of being kidnapped by Gauls and rescued by Vorenus and Pullo teaches him humility and the strength of the common soldier. From then on, he makes a point to stay on the soldiers' good side. Brutal Honesty: Uses this on occasion.

characters.name: Mark Antony
characters.description: Julius Caesar's right hand man and theoretical successor, Mark Antony is a soldier's soldier - violent, skilled, vicious, and enjoying a good time. At the series' start he is poised to march on Rome with Ceasar and serve as his second-in-command for the coming regime, and ends up playing a key part in his eventual success. However, his position dissolves when Caesar is killed, and he finds himself thrown into another decade of civil war with Octavian Caesar in an attempt to claim the throne. During this time, he periodically engages with Lucius Vorenus, one of his officers, and carries on an affair with Atia of the Julii, Caesar's cousin and effective head of the family besides him. While he eventually leaves for Egypt and meets his historical fate, he fulfills his role in history as a talented soldier who sought to grasp too much. Affably Evil: A hate-filled man of rapacious and self-aggrandizing instincts, but he's quite genial. His methods of intimidation, in particular, are always of the iron-fist-in-the-velvet-glove type. All Girls Want Bad Boys: Antony is a crude soldier at heart, and has a raw, animal magnetism that makes him extremely appealing to women. Anything That Moves: He's probably the person who has the most sex in the series, and in Roman fashion is open to sex with men as long as he's the one giving, not receiving.

characters.name: Atia of the Julii
characters.description: Niece of Julius, mother to Octavian, and long-time lover of Mark Antony, Atia of the Julii is closely positioned to the center of power in the era of Caesar. In contrast to the historical record, she is also violent, vain, promiscuous, egotistical, and selfish, stopping at nothing to uphold her family's position and wealth. At the series' start, she is Caesar's family member in the capital, which suits him well in holding court there. When the civil wars begin, she is kept safe as part of his family, and when he dies, she straddles the line between helping Mark Antony and helping her son in the struggle for power. A deliberate contradiction to every Roman womanly virtue, she stands as an antagonist for the other characters to strive against and sets in motion many schemes. Abusive Parents: Especially to Octavia, at first. She is always one to Octavian in favor of Antony which bites her back hard later on. Badass Boast: Besides the quote, there is also "By the five Furies, if I was not a gentle woman, I would have you flayed, and hung from a bracket at the door!" Bad Boss: Atia is very cruel to her slaves. In one scenes she's casually beating her slave Castor simply to vent her stress and irritation over Caesars rebellion. Bad Liar: Atia is an abysmal liar. For example, when Octavia asks if she's killed her husband her denial is incredibly false and theatrical.

characters.name: Octavia of the Julii
characters.description: Octavian's older sister andWoobiesupreme of the series. Being the only woman of marrying age in the family makes her the perfectUnwitting Pawnof Atia and later Octavian to gain political influence. Accidental Pornomancer: Poor Octavia. First there's her arranged marriage to Glabius, which she initially didn't want, but she ends up liking him. Then she has to get divorced from Glabius so that she can be offered to Pompey, who (in the Roman manner) has pre-marital anal sex with her but ends up marrying someone else. Then she falls for Servilia and has sex with her, who then coerces her tohave sex with her brotherfor information. In season two, much to her own surprise after being passed around so much, she falls for Agrippa but is forced to marry Mark Antony and bear his child.It's hinted in the finale that she and Agrippa might get back together. Arranged Marriage:Set up with Pompey and actually gone through with Antony. Big Sister Instinct: Most prominent when she rushes to comfort Octavian after he's choked by Antony, holding him while she screams at the older man and calls him an animal for his treatment of her brother. Sadly, it doesn't last, and by the end of the series she appears to despise him for his setting up of her and Atia to be humiliated by Antony. Bratty Teenage Daughter:Atiahilariously sees her as this.Octavia:Mother, I would rather it were not you who killed me. If you have no objections.Atia:Why ever not? You can't still be angry with me!

characters.name: Livia Drusilla
characters.description: Octavian's wife, a smart, no-nonsense young patrician girl who has no fear of challenging Atia. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: Appears in public as the perfect wife of a consul. Atia quickly and accurately pegs her as a power-hungry rival no different than herself. Brainy Brunette: In public events and even private dinners she seems somewhat ditzy. In private she is as clever as Octavian, quickly deducing his real goals and is even amused by his exploitation of his mother. Evil Counterpart: To Octavia. To her family (and especially her mother), Octavia is little more than a tool that can be used to forge political alliances and gain more power. Livia also marries for power, like Octavia, but it's her who decides to do so, and her mother does not control her in the least. Once married, Octavia is submissive and lets her husband decidewhat to do with her, while Livia soon reins Octavian in. Foil: While Cleopatra is accused of being a witch for being the more dominant partner in their relationship, everyone regards Livia as a nice lady when in private she is just as ruthless as Octavian.

characters.name: Calpurnia
characters.description: Arranged Marriage: Perfectly normal for a Roman woman, particularly a noblewoman, but not great when your husband has a libido you could hold up a house with. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome. Calpurnia is seen last in the season 2 premiere. This tracks with history, as little to nothing is said of her in any records after the immediate aftermath of Caesar's murder. Dreaming of Things to Come: Dreams of a flock ofcrows that form the shape of a skull.Caesardismisses the omen, saying he's been having dreams like that for years. Honor Before Reason: Will not leave Rome until Caesar's will has been read even as people could very well be looking to kill her. Ironically the will turns out to be more important than everyone thinks. I Choose to Stay: Antony is entirely willing to force her to come with them, because it will look bad politically if he flees Rome but leaves Caesar's wife behind. Ironically Antony ends up staying anyway when Octavian explains why the smarter move would be to stay put and Atia backs him up.

characters.name: Posca
characters.description: An old, wise and educated Greek slave owned by Caesar, who he tutored when he was young. Nowadays he acts as his personal manservant, counselor and confident. He is free to speak his mind, which includes a lot ofsnark. Almighty Janitor: To Caesar, being his slave but effectively acting as his personal confident, advisor and consigliere who handles Caesar's shady work such as distributing bribes or making deals with criminals so they will do Caesar's dirty work, thanks to his great intelligence and wisdow. Because You Were Nice to Me: His loyalty to Caesar comes largely from Caesar treating and loving him like family, and him genuinely valuing his advices and services. He ends up turning on Antony, who treats him very poorly and never shows any gratitude or respect to him. The Confidant: To Caesar, serving as hisSecret-Keeperabout Caesar's epileptic crisis, and being the one Caesar confides in when talking about his goals and motives. The Consigliere: He plays this role for Caesar, being his counselor and also handling the shady work such as bribing priests and other officials or criminals to do things the way Caesar want. Continues to play this role to Antony even after Caesar dies and frees him in his will. He even tries to educate Vorenus in the political game.

characters.name: Marcus Agrippa
characters.description: A young, humble Roman general of rural origins and old friend to Octavian. He loves Octavia, but his loyalty to her brother will never let him pursue her. Anguished Declaration of LoveAgrippa: I know you are kind, and full-hearted, and beautiful, and I would tear down the sky for you if you asked me to. Bad Liar: Is obviously anxious and uncomfortable while lying, and only gets away with it because most people don’t expect him to lie. He seems aware of his inability to lie convincingly because whenever someone asks him about something he’ll just bluntly state the plain truth upfront. Beware the Nice Ones: Agrippa is one of the kindest, most decent characters in the show. However, he's also the military powerhouse that paves the way for Octavian's victory. Blood Knight: Downplayed, but he clearly enjoys combat. When Mark Antony leads a cavalry charge at the Battle of Philippi, Agrippa watches the horsemen ride off longingly until Octavian notices and gives him leave to join the attack, much to Agrippa's glee.

characters.name: Gaius Maecenas
characters.description: Another friend of Octavian and third in the trio of young men that give birth to the Roman Empire. In contrast to Agrippa, Maecenas is a politician from a wealthy urban family who has a talent for rhetoric and his own personal goals beyond those of Octavian. Non-Action Guy: While Agrippa leads Octavian's armies in the field, Maecenas fights Octavian's battles on a different scene. Basically he runs the whole PR machine. Pretty Boy: Slender, young, attractive in a delicate, androgynous way. He’s seen maintaining this look by having servants pluck his body hair. Really Gets Around: He was bisexual in real life and is implied to be fitting this trope very well in the series, though we never actually see him in bed with anyone. Red Oni, Blue Oni: The blue to Agrippa’s red, being more logical, controlled, sly and pragmatic. He also almost always wears blue.

characters.name: Castor
characters.description: Head slave of the Julii Household. Ain't Too Proud to Beg: Grovels for mercy after he inadvertently lets a would-be assassin into the house, and only escapes gelding because Atia believes that eunuchs are not fashionable at the moment. Percussive Therapy: Atia is shown whipping him, not apparently because he's done anything wrong, but just to let off some steam. Undying Loyalty: Though he's never actually put to the test, so he could have just been diplomatic.Atia: And then you must kill yourself. Your survival would be most unsavory.Castor: I would not dream of living, domina.

characters.name: Merula
characters.description: An old female slave to the Julii, working mostly as a handmaiden to Atia. Even Evil Has Standards: As jaded and accustomed to her mistress' cruetly shes shown to be extremely disturbed and horrified by Servilias screams as shes tortured by Atias men. Evil Old Folks: In an unquestioning servant of her mistress' schemes. Happiness in Slavery: Very devoted to Atia. Ninja Maid: When Antony strikes her mistress, she quickly pulls a knife on him. Antony is naked and unarmed, so doesn't dispute the point.

characters.name: Glabius
characters.description: The first husband of Octavia. Buy Them Off: Tries to do this to Timon, who unfortunately is more loyal to Atia. Happily Married: To Octavia, until Atia forces a divorce on them so her daughter free to marry Pompeii. It'sAll for Nothingas Pompey was never interested in going through with it, marrying the daughter of a senator opposed to Caesar instead. Make It Look Like an Accident: Or rather a random act of violence during a time ofCivil War. Murder the Hypotenuse: Even after Pompey marries, Atia refuses to allow Octavia to go back to Glabius, so her daughter will be free to marry someone else. When she discovers that her daughter is secretly visiting Glabius, she sends Timon to murder him.

characters.name: Julia
characters.description: Daughter of Caesar and the previous wife of Pompey. Morality Chain: Julia was the only who could keep the peace between father and her husband. Everything falls apart after she dies. This isTruth in Television. Perfectly Arranged Marriage: Though she and Pompey married as an alliance between Caesar and Pompey, they eventually fell in love or at least grew to care for each other. According to historical records they were all over each other to the extent that it became a minor scandal. Plot-Triggering Death: Julia's death accelerates the decay of Caesar and Pompeii's friendship.

characters.name: Marcus Junius Brutus
characters.description: A quiet and awkward young Patrician torn between doing what he believes it's right and what others expect of him - particularly hismother Serviliaand hisParental Substitute, Julius Caesar. The Alcoholic: Hits the jar in the second season. All for Nothing: All of his opposition against Caesar was in the name of saving the Republic from tyranny. Not only did Caesar gain absolute power over Rome, but his assassinating Caesar just paved the way for worse tyrants like Octavian to put the final nail in the Republic’s coffin for good. Apologetic Attacker: Looks to be quite remorseful before delivering the killing blow to Caesar Crouching Moron, Hidden Badass: Starts off ignorant and uninterested in politics but when pushed too far he proves himself to be handy with a knife and capable of leading an army into battle.

characters.name: Servilia of the Junii
characters.description: Brutus' mother and long time mistress to Julius Caesar. Serviliaisa bitter and evil woman, but her dangerousness always falls one level short of her main rival, Atia of the Julii. Antagonist in Mourning: If you look closely, when her son informs her that Caesar is dead she sheds aSingle Tear. She then turns up inWidow's Weedsto view his body, despite being spat on by his widow. When she views the body however, her expression is carefully masked so we don't know if she's happy or regretful. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: Plays the poor victim very well but often is just as vicious and manipulative as her enemies. Blatant Lies: When Brutus calls her out for stirring opposition against Caesar because he ended their relationship, she claims her efforts are purely to oppose tyranny, and aren’t personal at all. Cold-Blooded Torture: Is subjected to this at the hands of Atia.

characters.name: Cato the Younger
characters.description: A stubborn, staunch traditionalist senator and defender of the Republic, and as a result, a declared enemy of Caesar. Also half-brother to Servilia and uncle to Brutus. Age Lift: TheReal LifeCato was actually younger than Caesar. Alas, Poor Villain: He gains little to no sympathy in his intransigent feud against Caesar, fatally dragging friends and countrymen into civil war, yet his demise remains both pathetic and tragic. Be Careful What You Wish For: He’s the most vocal in pushing the senate into open conflict with Caesar. Pompey unhappily congratulates him when a mob prevents Antony from vetoing a political stunt to declare Caesar an enemy of the Republic, and ensuringCivil War. Commander Contrarian: Makes a career out of it.

characters.name: Gaius Cassius Longinus
characters.description: Another fellow senator and close friend of Brutus. He masterminds Caesar's assassination. All for Nothing: He is one of the chief conspirators in Caesar’s assassination, and encourages Brutus into participating under the nominal justification of saving the republic from tyranny, but also in preventing the patrician class from losing power. Caesar’s assassination not only fails to save the republic from tyranny, but Octavian also makes sure to murder a large portion of the wealthy and patrician class before his ascension to eliminate the same threat they posed to Caesar. Cassandra Truth: Warns Brutus that Antony is too dangerous to live and must be killed regardless of honor. Brutus refuses to listen and Antony soon rouses the people against them, then defeats their forces in the subsequentCivil War. Died on Their Birthday: Just before commencing the Battle of Phillipi, Brutus remembers it's Cassius's birthday and wishes him a happy one. Cassius had honestly forgotten this, and good-naturedly forgives Brutus for the absence of a cake. When the battle turns against them, Cassius leads the reserve unit into the chaos, and is brought back mortally wounded.Brutus: Cassius, what happened?Cassius:(coughing)Not sure, to be honest.Hell of a birthday... Fatal Family Photo: Or "Fatal Promise of a Birthday Cake for Next Year".

characters.name: Eleni
characters.description: A slave property of Servilia. Happiness in Slavery: To Servilia, willingly assisting her in her vendetta against Caesar. Shipper on Deck: Assures Servilia that Caesar truly loves heruntil he demonstrates otherwise. Together in Death:After his mistress kills herself as a human sacrifice, she willingly follows suit. Undying Loyalty: To the point ofcommitting suicide after her mistress does.

characters.name: Pompey
characters.description: Once a mighty conqueror and hero of the Republic, Pompey is a jaded Consul of Rome, living mostly on past glories by the time the show starts. When Caesar tries to seize power he sides with the Senate against him. Alas, Poor Villain: Pompey is little worse than his rivals and more antagonistic than truly villainous, yet he succumbs to the prodding of the senatorial faction, turning against his former kin and friend, Caesar. His fall is both tragic and pitiable. Always Someone Better: He's a great general in his own right but ultimately can't hold a candle to Caesar. This bothers him a lot. Arranged Marriage: His marriage to Julia, daughter of Caesar, is specifically pointed as the only thing that makes the two get along. HerDeath by Childbirthsets the show in motion. Later, an attempt to solve their conflict by making him marry Octavia falls through. Boring, but Practical: His solid strategy to defeat Caesar by attrition in Greece works perfectly, but the senators argue that it is not an adequate way of winning and convince Pompey that he must deliver the decisive blow in battle. This leads to their defeat at Pharsalus and the loss of the civil war.

characters.name: Quintus Valerius Pompey
characters.description: Pompey's son and a military leader in the Senate faction. Composite Character: He is loosely based on Pompey's two historical sons, Gnaeus Pompey the Younger and Sextus Pompey. Deadly Hug:After seeing Brutus publicly embrace Antony, he's confused when Antony makes a show of approaching him also with hand outstretched in friendship. Until Antony cuts his throat with the knife hidden in his other hand. The Dragon: To his father, who sends him to recover the missing Treasury gold. Unfortunately his hired thugs are less than reliable and he gets captured by Pullo and Vorenus. Drowning My Sorrows: Becomes one after his father's death and defeat. He quickly bounces back after Servilia takes him into her household and makes him her ally.

characters.name: Cornelia
characters.description: Scipio's daughter and Pompey's second wife. Establishing Character Moment/Proper Lady: We first meet her accompanying her father to the theatre. She tactfully requests that he take her away when she realizes that the production includes tasteless nudity. The Promise: That she and her family will return. They never do. What Happened to the Mouse?: She and Pompey's children are last seen witnessing him getting killed. Historically she survived and lived out the rest of her life peacefully in Rome.

characters.name: Erastes Fulmen
characters.description: A ruthless businessman who controls Rome's most powerful criminal gang, the Aventinecollegium, under a pretense of legality. All Devouring Black Hole Loan Sharks: Thoughhe refuses to extend credit to Vorenus, citing this trope, he has no issue with using him as a paid enforcer to collect his own debts. Attack! Attack... Retreat! Retreat!: Erastes turns up with his gang at Vorenus' house to murder him, only to findCaesar's personal guardstanding outside. He beats a hasty retreat.Unfortunately he doesn't forget his grudge and returns to the house after hearing of Caesar's death. Bad Boss: Erastes is furious that Vorenus slapped his man, because onlyheis allowed to chastise his own men,which he promptly demonstrates by breaking a vase over the man's head. Decapitation Presentation:An enraged Vorenus chops off his head and keeps it as a trophy.

characters.name: Gaia
characters.description: A former brothel supervisor tasked with keeping the customers on line, who is hired by Vorenus afterhe takes over Erastes Fulmen's gang. While technically a slave, Gaia is a tough and vindictive woman that stops at nothing to get what she wants. Action Girl: Can certainly hold her own in a fight and takes part in a mob war. Belligerent Sexual Tension: Tries it with Vorenus, and afternot getting a response, with Pullo with more success. Conveniently Timed Attack from Behind: In aDramatic Ironyshe's fatally stabbed after saving Pullo's life, only to be strangled by him on her deathbed. Deathbed Confession:After being wounded in battle, she confesses to Pullo that it was her who murdered Eirene. Pullo suffocates her and throws her body in the river. To the Ancient Romans, this was a way ofdamning someone. Without proper funeral rites, their soul would be unable to enter the underworld and they would be stuck in limbo for all eternity.

characters.name: Mascius
characters.description: A former legionary of the Thirteenth. After returning from Gaul, Vorenus gets him and his other comrades lands in Germania but Mascius loses his concession playing dice. He then returns to Rome and joins the Aventine gang after hearing that Vorenus has taken over it. And Zoidberg: Vorenus makes him third-in-command beneath Pullo, much to his annoyance as Mascius used to outrank him."Oh I get it, third fucking man and all that!" Bash Brothers: Read the quote. Don't Tell Mama: He asks Vorenus not to tell his family what he does for a living. Dude, Where's My Respect?: Does not take the fact that Vorenus returns to the original hierarchy of the gang after he and Pullo returns, especially as Mascius had been de facto leader of the gang while Vorenus had joined Antony on campaign.

characters.name: Memmio
characters.description: Captain of the Caelians gang and uneasy ally to Vorenus. Beard of Barbarism: After beinglocked in a cageand starved. The Dog Bites Back: He eventually escapes his cageand would have succeeded in killing Pullo if it weren't for Gaia. Humiliation Conga: Pullo bites off his tongue, keeps him in a cage and feeds him on scrapsas a message for others. Lousy Lovers Are Losers: When Vorenus comes to see him about the theft of Herod's gold, Memmio isdiscovered in a back room taking a blindfolded and nude female slave from behind. He doesn't seem to be enjoying it much and neither apparently is she, and when his meeting is over he doesn't even go back to finish off, preferring to sit and drink with his men.

characters.name: Marcus Tullius Cicero
characters.description: The renownedRoman politician, intellectual and master orator, leader of the moderate faction in the Senate. Cicero distrusts Caesar's and later Mark Antony's actions but lacks the spine (and, to be fair, the military muscle) to take direct action against them. Arch-Enemy: To Mark Antony. Blackmail Backfire: Downplayed. He does tentatively float the possibility of paying his assassin a very generous amount of money to let him live, but is clearly not surprised when Pullo turns him down, as it wouldn't be worth it. It was clearly a last-ditch "might as well try" move rather than something Cicero was genuinely hoping would succeed. Compelling Voice: The orator of the ancient world, and arguably one of the greatest public speakers who has ever lived. Cowardly Lion: He's aNon-Action Guyand not physically brave, but in the end he stands up to Antony and faces his death with dignity.

characters.name: Metellus Scipio
characters.description: A prominent general in the senate faction and member of the famous Scipio family. He becomes Pompey's father-in-law in the first episode. Driven to Suicide: Has his throat cut. The Pollyanna: Cato describes him as having a "tolerant spirit" and he stays hopeful for the Republican cause after everyone else on his side has died, surrendered to Caesar or given up hope. Cato's suicide finally breaks him. Satellite Character: He is an extension of Cato most of the time, with little narrative autonomy. Those Two Guys: In a way, with Cato.

characters.name: Jocasta
characters.description: A young Plebeian woman, daughter of a rich merchant and close friend of Octavia. Brainless Beauty: Comes off as rather ditzy most of the time. Cloudcuckoolander: Bubbly and cheerful but very strange, prone to non-sequiturs and saying whatever pops into her head, which tends to be a bit random and peculiar even when she’s not very very stoned. She occasionally plays it up to get away with things. Defiled Forever: States this after her rape, and she's forced to marry lower-class Posca as a result. However she bounces back remarkably. Fallen Princess: Jocasta's parents are killed and their money stolen.

characters.name: Lepidus
characters.description: A Roman General sent by the Senate to defeat Antony, only to have his entire army defect to him on the spot. He then switches sides too and ends up as Antony's second in command. He later becomes the least powerful of the Triumvirate formed with Antony and Octavian, but gets Africa for his troubles... for a while. Adaptational Wimp: In real life, Lepidus was one of Caesar's close allies and an able commander and politician in his own right, but had the misfortune ofliving at the same time as Antony and Octavian. Here, he is very clearly theButt-Monkeyto the other triumvirs. Butt-Monkey: To Antony and Octavian. A Father to His Men: Averted; which Antony suggests is why his troops were so willing to defect. General Failure: His entire army defects to Antony. Antony however just uses this to co-opt him to his side.

characters.name: The Newsreader
characters.description: A public servant that cries news andadvertisementsat the Forum in front of the Roman Senate. Bookends: He appears in both the pilot and the last episode making the same announcement, offering a reward for a missing slave, "runaway or stolen". It's used to show that despite the vast historical changes happened over the span of the series, the life of the average citizen remains the same. Catchphrase: Even he gets tired of saying,"True Roman bread, for true Romans." The Constant: Survives all the turmoil of civil wars and public strife. Kent Brockman News: In his defense his scripts are written by others.

characters.name: Queen Cleopatra
characters.description: The infamous last Hellenistic monarch of Egypt, a beautiful and ambitious woman that is well aware of her powers of seduction. 0% Approval Rating: By the Romans, who believe she's a sorceress who's swayed Mark Antony from decent Roman ways. Adaptational Attractiveness: As per all popular depictions of Cleopatra. Ambition Is Evil: Cleopatra isn't content with ruling Egypt and tries to take her son's legacy, with warring results. Aroused by Their Voice: Cleopatra purposely uses a higher pitched voice in order to seduce men. She's also just as capable as striking fear into her little brother and his lickspittles with her deeper voice, as well. The real Cleopatra was described to have a very charming and alluring voice, and it is said that this was one of her more attractive features rather than simply being incredibly beautiful.

characters.name: King Ptolemy XIII
characters.description: The naïve child king of Egypt, younger brother and husband to Cleopatra. 13 Is Unlucky: Ptolemy XIII is the last Ptolemy to truly rule Eygpt (two others with the name were technically Pharaoh, his younger brother then Caesarion, whose real name was also Ptolemy, but both were Cleopatra's puppets). Adipose Rex: On the way to becoming one. Arranged Marriage: To Cleopatra. Asshole Victim: Given how much of aRoyal Brathe is, it's hard to feel sorry for his fate.

characters.name: Caesarion
characters.description: Prince of Egypt, son of Cleopatra and (allegedly) Caesar. Appeal to Flattery: While fleeing Octavian asKing Incognito, a Roman soldiermakes him reveal himselfby asking for his blessing in Egyptian. Consider that at no point did the Roman guards hide their identity. Baby Trap: Cleopatra has him so she can have a claim on Rome.To ensure having a child in the first place, she hedges her bets by having lots of sex with the nearest available Roman, who happens to be Pullo. Beethoven Was an Alien Spy:Caesarion was Titus Pullo's son and successfully escaped Octavian to live as a plebeian in Rome. Big Eater: He doesn't show it often. But he eats more than a black hole, despite being rail thin.

characters.name: Charmian
characters.description: A trusted slave in the service of Cleopatra. Brutal Honesty: Cleopatra hits her and can order her death at a whim, but she refuses to guard her tongue. It's obvious that they have the kind of relationship where she can get away with this honesty however. Foil: Charmian is the female Posca. Parental Substitute: To Cleopatra. Servile Snarker: As with Posca, her position enables her to get away with saying things that would get others executed.

characters.name: Pothinus
characters.description: The Egyptian Prime Minister and regent to King Ptolemy. He goads the young monarch against his older sister-wife, Cleopatra. Played by Tony Guilfoyle. Ambiguously Gay: It might not be the case in the original version, but the Spanish dub makes him flamboyant. Wearingguylinerdoesn't help, though that's entirely down to fashion. Bad Liar: Andhe thinkshe can foolCaesarandPosca... Butt-Monkey: Is used a kicking boy during a game of blindman's bluff with his king. As such he doesn't notice Caesar and aNot Quite DeadCleopatra entering the throne room behind him. He's then offered up asThe Scapegoatfor ordering Cleopatra's execution.He then tries to put the blame on Theodotus instead.Both of them end up on a spike. Culture Clash: He kills Pompey and presents his head to Caesar thinking that it will gain his trust. Caesar is enraged instead.

characters.name: Theodotus of Chíos
characters.description: Ptolemy XIII's Greek rhetoric tutor. Bad Boss: Sends Septimius to deliver a message to Caesaridentifying him as Pompey's killer. Death by Adaptation: Type II. InReal Lifehe escaped the war in Egypt and was murdered later in Asia Minor by Brutus. In the show, he isexecuted along with Pothinus. Guyliner: As per Egyptian fashion. It Will Never Catch On: What he thinks about Rome and its power.

characters.name: Achillas
characters.description: The commander of the Egyptian army loyal to Ptolemy XIII. Appeal to Flattery: He is reluctant to attack Caesar andgain Rome's wrath as a result. Theodotus makes him act by questioning his popularity. Doomed by Canon: His fate is unrevealed and the war against Caesar happens off-screen, but you know he won't prevail.noteIn Real Life, he was murdered by Cleopatra's younger sister Arsinoe, who supported Ptolemy, and replaced as commander of the army by her lover, Ganymedes. Know When to Fold 'Em: He's worried about Rome's military power, and as such is very relucdant to go against Caesar, knowing that killing Caesar would not be forgotten nor forgiven by the Romans. The other Egyptian leaders don't listen to him. Only Sane Man: He's more cautious and level-headed than Ptolemy's other advisors. When Theodotus suggests to him that he attacks and kill Caesar, Achillas is worried about Rome's reaction, knowing that Rome's military power is very formidable and that such an action would draw Rome's retaliation. And when Theodotus tells him to forget about Rome, saying that it's on the brinf of ruin and will be destroyed in ten years, he's visibly not buying it and says that even if it happens there would still be enough time for the Romans to retaliate against them. His worries are ignored by the others.

characters.name: Lucius Septimius
characters.description: A Roman veteran serving as mercenary in the Egyptian Army. Bodyguard Betrayal: He murders Pompey (under whom he served) when he comes to Egypt seeking refuge. Death by Adaptation: The fate of the real Septimius is unknown. Decapitation Presentation: He beheads Pompey after killing him.And is in turn beheaded by Caesar. Forced to Watch: Kills Pompey within sight of his wife and children.

characters.name: Tevye a.k.a. Timon
characters.description: A largely secularized Jewish horse trader (at least when he is among non believers) who has lived for decades in Rome and often serves as a mercenary for Atia, be it as a hitman or a bodyguard. He also expresses a strong lust for her, preferring to be paid in sex rather than money. Played by Lee Boardman. Accidental Murder:Accidentally kills Levi during a knife struggle with him, in an effort to stop him from assassinating Herod. Beard of Evil: The goatee version. Bodyguard Betrayal: He finally breaks andattacks Atiawhen she tells him tomutilateServilia. Bodyguard Crush: On Atia.

characters.name: Levi
characters.description: Timon's brother, azealotthat moves to Rome in the second season after getting in trouble in Judea.He is actually in asuicide missionto kill King Herod during a visit to the city. Aborted Arc: It's obvious that Levi was meant to stay around for a whole season and thathis deathwould have some influence on Timon's later development, given that the show was expected to explore Judeain a fifth seasonand that Timon is last seen travelling there. Big Brother Bully: Before his ownHeel–Faith Turn. Timon still harbors resentment that his brother used to bully him, yet is now lecturing him on his lifestyle. The Fundamentalist: As a member of the Zealots, which makes him a declared enemy of King Herod because he is a Roman imposed puppet. Hoist by His Own Petard:Tries to get his brother to rediscover his Judaism by joining him in killing Herod. Timon has doubts, they struggle, and Levi ends stabbed by the knife he planned to use to kill his enemy.

characters.name: Herod
characters.description: YesthatHerod, the client-King of Judea. Aborted Arc: As part of Timon and Levi's storyline in the second season, his involvement in this was cut to a minimum. Herod would probably have appeared again if the show had reached the five seasons envisioned by Heller,with the last one depicting Judea immediately before the birth of Christ. Puppet King: Of Judea, to the Romans. The Quisling: Herod has no desire to break with Rome and is quite effective at navigating Roman Politics as a subservient client-King. As a result of this subservience, he is targeted for assassination as a by Jewish fanatics. Real Men Wear Pink: Specifically, jewels in his hair and...
